title: Modular Lower Bounds on Reeh-Schlieder State Preparation
abstract: The Reeh-Schlieder theorem says that every target vector can be approximated from the vacuum by an operator localized in an arbitrarily small spacetime region, but it gives no quantitative cost for doing so. This note isolates a standard Tomita-Takesaki estimate as a model-independent preparation bound. Targets with deeply negative modular energy require large local operators. After rescaling such an operator to a physical contraction, the same estimate becomes a lower bound on postselection overhead. In geometries where the modular Hamiltonian is known, the bound becomes explicit. Bisognano-Wichmann turns it into a boost energy statement for wedges, and the Casini-Huerta-Myers formula gives a stress-tensor version for bounded regions of conformal field theories. Local unitaries can only reach states of nonnegative modular energy. Negative modular sectors require nonunitary or postselected outcomes, giving a preparation cost bound that complements vacuum embezzlement in type III local algebras.
summary: This paper provides a quantitative limit on the Reeh-Schlieder theorem by showing that while any state can be prepared locally, states with negative modular energy impose an exponential cost on the required operator strength and measurement success probability. It establishes that local unitary dynamics cannot access the negative modular sector of a quantum field.
main_problem: Quantifying the operational cost, in terms of operator norm and postselection probability, required to prepare target states from the vacuum using local operators in Quantum Field Theory.
main_result: The authors derive a modular lower bound showing that preparing states with negative modular energy requires an exponential increase in operator norm and postselection overhead. They specifically show that local unitaries are restricted to states with non-negative modular energy.
method: The study utilizes Algebraic Quantum Field Theory (AQFT) frameworks, specifically Tomita-Takesaki theory, to relate the norm of local operators to the expectation value of the modular Hamiltonian.
model_or_system: The framework is based on local algebras (Type III factors) in Minkowski space, focusing on specific geometries such as Rindler wedges and causal diamonds in Conformal Field Theories (CFTs).
key_observables: Operator norm of the local operator, modular energy (expectation value of the modular Hamiltonian), and postselection success probability.
important_parameters: The regime of negative modular energy, the weight/lapse function in CFT balls, and the boost energy in Rindler wedges.
main_assumptions: The explicit form of the modular Hamiltonian is assumed to be known for specific geometries (wedges and CFT balls); the results are derived within the context of von Neumann algebras.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of quantifying Reeh-Schlieder costs, develops a model-independent bound using Tomita-Takesaki theory, applies this to specific geometric cases (wedges and CFTs), and concludes with the implications for local unitaries and postselection.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
